mnemonics: Please Do Not Take Salami Pizza Away (for the OSI model layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
keyPoints: The OSI model is a conceptual framework used to understand network interactions.
TCP/IP is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet.
Routers operate at the Network Layer (Layer 3) and are responsible for path determination and packet forwarding.
commonMistakes: Confusing the OSI model with the TCP/IP model, which has four layers instead of seven.
Assuming a Router is a Layer 2 device (like a switch), when it actually operates at Layer 3.
Believing the Transport Layer (Layer 4) is responsible for encryption, when that is the role of the Presentation Layer (Layer 6).
